name: Alan King
blurb: He made dozens of appearances on The Ed Sullivan Show, which launched his national career, and later served as longtime host of the New York Friars Club roasts.
bio: Alan King was an American comedian known for his biting, conversational monologues about marriage, suburbia, and the everyday frustrations of middle-class life, a style that became a blueprint for modern observational stand-up. He made dozens of appearances on The Ed Sullivan Show, which launched his national career, and later served as longtime host of the New York Friars Club roasts.

timeline.ev: Performs as a teenager in the Catskills Borscht Belt
First appearance on The Ed Sullivan Show
Performs at President Kennedy inaugural ball
Plays union boss Andy Stone in Martin Scorsese film Casino
Dies of lung cancer in New York City

accolades: Longtime host and historian of the New York Friars Club roasts
Founded the Alan King Award in American Jewish Humor

The Tonight Show Starring Johnny Carson

flags.bits_honest_gap_note: No NEW oembed-verified OFFICIAL clip found beyond the existing Carson bit (32GeTDmKTRI). Ed Sullivan archival performances (Doctors / Top Man Doctors) surface only on personal channels (e.g. author_name 'Hans Raeymaekers', OQfHi5hIsAM) or as dead/unembeddable IDs (oXPGISyqLAQ returns Not Found); the official The Ed Sullivan Show / UMe channel has no Alan King video that surfaced and embeds. No 'Alan King - Topic' album-track channel exists for the comedian (the '- Topic' hits are an unrelated blues musician 'King King' / singer-songwriter). Links: he died 2004; no official estate/site exists (alankingproject.com is a different Alan King, a musician). Honest gap for both the 2 added bits and the 2 links rather than ship a personal-channel re-upload or a wrong site.

ranking_notes: Matthew flagged the existing 1:20:00 clip as suspect. The 1988 Letterman set is a strong tight replacement, though the upload is third-party (oembed returns empty author_name). Use as link-out 'Watch' rather than embed if a stricter official source can't be found. Defer final swap to Matthew.
